segments.name: option
segments.comment: Configuration option where the current route will be stored

segments.name: channel
segments.comment: Bus channel. Note: as the SWB is currently implemented by a crossbar, the channel is always equals to the target slot.

segments.name: option
segments.comment: Configuration option where the current route will be stored

segments.name: sr
segments.comment: Sending route (`0`) or a receiving route (`1`)

segments.name: source
segments.comment: If `sr=0`, indicates source slot number; if `sr=1`, indicates receiving direction (0:NW/1:N/2:NE/3:W/4:C/5:E/6:SW/7:S/8:SE).

segments.name: target
segments.comment: If `sr=0`, indicates sending direction (bits 0 to 8: NW/N/NE/W/C/E/SW/S/SE); if `sr=1`, indicates destination slot(s) (1-hot encoded)

segments.name: port
segments.comment: Selects whether the event is applied intracell (0) or intercell (1).

segments.name: port
segments.comment: Selects whether the repetition is applied intracell (0) or intercell (1).

segments.name: port
segments.comment: Selects whether the repetition extension is applied intracell (0) or intercell (1).

segments.name: iter
segments.comment: Most significant bits to apply to the `iter` value of the repetition operator.

segments.name: step
segments.comment: Most significant bits to apply to the `step` value of the repetition operator.

segments.name: delay
segments.comment: Most significant bits to apply to the `delay` value of the repetition operator.

segments.name: port
segments.comment: Selects whether the transition is applied intracell (0) or intercell (2).
